top_winning_behaviors.insight: Setting explicit next steps creates accountability and momentum that drives prospects to commit to demos
top_winning_behaviors.booked_rate: 47 of 50 booked calls had clear next steps
top_winning_behaviors.evidence_stat: 94% of booked calls vs 55% of not booked calls

top_winning_behaviors.insight: Successful reps invest time to build rapport and understand needs rather than rushing to pitch
top_winning_behaviors.evidence_stat: 64% longer than not booked calls (3.9 min)

top_winning_behaviors.insight: Following up with warm leads who already showed interest converts at the highest rate
top_winning_behaviors.booked_rate: 29 of 50 booked calls used this angle
top_winning_behaviors.evidence_stat: 58% of successful calls vs 19% of unsuccessful calls

top_objections.frequency: Appears in multiple forms across booked calls
top_objections.best_response: Acknowledge the concern but emphasize the demo will show ROI specific to their business
top_objections.example_rep_behavior: Still managed to book despite pricing pushback by focusing on value demonstration

top_objections.frequency: Most common in not booked calls
top_objections.best_response: Probe deeper into specific timing concerns rather than accepting surface-level rejection
top_objections.example_rep_behavior: Ask follow-up questions to uncover the real barriers

top_objections.best_response: Position the solution as something that reduces their workload rather than adds to it
top_objections.example_rep_behavior: Frame demo as time-saving rather than time-consuming

opening_angle_analysis.insight: Warm followups with prospects who already expressed interest convert at the highest rate because intent is pre-established
opening_angle_analysis.worst_insight: Leading with cost savings creates price-focused conversations that commoditize the solution rather than building value

tenure_insight: Experience isn't the primary differentiator - mid-tenure reps actually have the highest booking rate at 32.8% vs 28.6% for new reps and 38.0% for senior reps. This suggests there's a sweet spot where reps have enough experience to be confident but haven't become complacent, and that proper training and process adherence matter more than years of experience.
duration_insight: Call duration is a leading indicator of success, with booked calls lasting 64% longer than unsuccessful ones. This isn't about talking more, but about investing time upfront to understand prospect needs, build rapport, and properly qualify opportunities. The extra 2.5 minutes allows reps to move beyond surface-level pitching into consultative selling that creates genuine interest in seeing a demo.

three_things_we_didnt_know.title: Social Proof Backfires Spectacularly
three_things_we_didnt_know.detail: 77 of 99 unsuccessful calls used social proof vs only 18 of 50 successful calls, suggesting generic testimonials actually hurt conversion when overused.

three_things_we_didnt_know.title: Discovery Questions Don't Drive Demos
three_things_we_didnt_know.detail: 66 of 99 unsuccessful calls asked discovery questions vs 29 of 50 successful calls, proving that questioning without purpose creates interrogation rather than connection.

three_things_we_didnt_know.title: No Objections Means Better Outcomes
three_things_we_didnt_know.detail: Half of booked calls (25 of 50) had zero objections vs only 33% of unsuccessful calls, indicating strong qualification prevents resistance rather than creates it.
